author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm II

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Why do the heathen rage, and the people meditate vain things?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The kings of the earth have stood up, and the rulers taken counsel together, against the Lord, and against His Christ” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is said, “why?” as if it were said, in vain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what they wished, namely, Christ’s destruction, they accomplished not; for this is spoken of our Lord’s persecutors, of whom also mention is made in the Acts of the Apostles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let us break their bonds asunder, and cast away their yoke from us” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although it admits of another acceptation, yet is it more fitly understood as in the person of those who are said to “meditate vain things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So that “let us break their bonds asunder, and cast away their yoke from us,” may be, let us do our endeavour, that the Christian religion do not bind us, nor be imposed upon us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He that dwelleth in the heavens shall laugh them to scorn, and the Lord shall have them in derision” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sentence is repeated; for “He who dwelleth in the heavens,” is afterwards put, “the Lord;” and for “shall laugh them to scorn,” is afterwards put, “shall have them in derision.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing of this however must be taken in a carnal sort, as if God either laugheth with cheek, or derideth with nostril; but it is to be understood of that power which He giveth to His saints, that they seeing things to come, namely, that the Name and rule of Christ is to pervade posterity and possess all nations, should understand that those men “meditate a vain thing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this power whereby these things are foreknown is God’s “laughter” and “derision.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He that dwelleth in the heavens shall laugh them to scorn.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If by “heavens” we understand holy souls, by these God, as foreknowing what is to come, will “laugh them to scorn, and have them in derision.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then He shall speak unto them in His wrath, and vex them in His sore displeasure” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For showing more clearly how He will “speak unto them,” he added, He will “vex them;” so that “in His wrath,” is, “in His sore displeasure.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But by the “wrath and sore displeasure” of the Lord God must not be understood any mental perturbation; but the might whereby He most justly avengeth, by the subjection of all creation to His service.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that is to be observed and remembered which is written in the Wisdom of Solomon, “But Thou, Lord of power, judgest with tranquillity, and with great favour orderest us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “wrath” of God then is an emotion which is produced in the soul which knoweth the law of God, when it sees this same law transgressed by the sinner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by this emotion of righteous souls many things are avenged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although the “wrath” of God can be well understood of that darkening of the mind, which overtakes those who transgress the law of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yet am I set by Him as King upon Sion, His holy hill, preaching His decree” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is clearly spoken in the Person of the very Lord our Saviour Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if Sion signify, as some interpret, beholding, we must not understand it of anything rather than of the Church, where daily is the desire raised of beholding the bright glory of God, according to that of the Apostle, “but we with open face beholding the glory of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the meaning of this is, Yet I am set by Him as King over His holy Church; which for its eminence and stability He calleth a mountain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yet I am set by Him as King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I, that is, whose “bands” they were meditating “to break asunder,” and whose “yoke” to “cast away.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Preaching His decree.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who doth not see the meaning of this, seeing it is daily practised?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath said unto me, Thou art My Son, to-day have I begotten Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although that day may also seem to be prophetically spoken of, on which Jesus Christ was born according to the flesh; and in eternity there is nothing past as if it had ceased to be, nor future as if it were not yet, but present only, since whatever is eternal, always is; yet as “today” intimates presentiality, a divine interpretation is given to that expression, “To-day have I begotten Thee,” whereby the uncorrupt and Catholic faith proclaims the eternal generation of the power and Wisdom of God, who is the Only-begotten Son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Ask of Me, and I shall give Thee the nations for Thine inheritance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This has at once a temporal sense with reference to the Manhood which He took on Himself, who offered up Himself as a Sacrifice in the stead of all sacrifices, who also maketh intercession for us; so that the words, “ask of Me,” may be referred to all this temporal dispensation, which has been instituted for mankind, namely, that the “nations” should be joined to the Name of Christ, and so be redeemed from death, and possessed by God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I shall give Thee the nations for Thine inheritance,” which so possess them for their salvation, and to bear unto Thee spiritual fruit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the uttermost parts of the earth for Thy possession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same repeated, “The uttermost parts of the earth,” is put for “the nations;” but more clearly, that we might understand all the nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And “Thy possession” stands for “Thine inheritance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt rule them with a rod of iron,” with inflexible justice, and “Thou shalt break them like a potter’s vessel” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9); that is, “Thou shalt break” in them earthly lusts, and the filthy doings of the old man, and whatsoever hath been derived and inured from the sinful clay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And now understand, ye kings” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And now;” that is, being now renewed, your covering of clay worn out, that is, the carnal vessels of error which belong to your past life, “now understand,” ye who now are “kings;” that is, able now to govern all that is servile and brutish in you, able now too to fight, not as “they who beat the air, but chastening your bodies, and bringing them into subjection.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be instructed, all ye who judge the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This again is a repetition; “Be instructed” is instead of “understand;” and “ye who judge the earth” instead of “ye kings.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He signifies the spiritual by “those who judge the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whatsoever we judge, is below us; and whatsoever is below the spiritual man, is with good reason called “the earth;” because it is defiled with earthly corruption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Serve the Lord with fear;” lest what is said, “Ye kings and judges of the earth,” turn into pride: “And rejoice with trembling” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Very excellently is “rejoice” added, lest “serve the Lord with fear” should seem to tend to misery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But again, lest this same rejoicing should run on to unrestrained inconsiderateness, there is added “with trembling,” that it might avail for a warning, and for the careful guarding of holiness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It can also be taken thus, “And now ye kings understand;” that is, And now that I am set as King, be ye not sad, kings of the earth, as if your excellency were taken from you, but rather “understand and be instructed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is expedient for you, that ye should be under Him, by whom understanding and instruction are given you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is expedient for you, that ye lord it not with rashness, but that ye “serve the Lord” of all “with fear,” and “rejoice” in bliss most sure and most pure, with all caution and carefulness, lest ye fall therefrom into pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lay hold of discipline, lest at any time the Lord be angry, and ye perish from the righteous way” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the same as, “understand,” and, “be instructed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to understand and be instructed, this is to lay hold of discipline.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Still in that it is said, “lay hold of,” it is plainly enough intimated that there is some protection and defence against all things which might do hurt unless with so great carefulness it be laid hold of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lest at any time the Lord be angry,” is expressed with a doubt, not as regards the vision of the prophet to whom it is certain, but as regards those who are warned; for they, to whom it is not openly revealed, are wont to think with doubt of the anger of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then they ought to say to themselves, let us “lay hold of discipline, lest at any time the Lord be angry, and we perish from the righteous way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now, how “the Lord be angry” is to be taken, has been said above.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And “ye perish from the righteous way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a great punishment, and dreaded by those who have had any perception of the sweetness of righteousness; for he who perisheth from the way of righteousness, in much misery will wander through the ways of unrighteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When His anger shall be shortly kindled, blessed are all they who put their trust in Him;” that is, when the vengeance shall come which is prepared for the ungodly and for sinners, not only will it not light on those “who put their trust in” the Lord, but it will even avail for the foundation and exaltation of a kingdom for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he said not, “When His anger shall be shortly kindled,” safe “are all they who put their trust in Him,” as though they should have this only thereby, to be exempt from punishment; but he said, “blessed;” in which there is the sum and accumulation of all good things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the meaning of “shortly” I suppose to be this, that it will be something sudden, whilst sinners will deem it far off and long to come.
